from.note: On-demand local LoRA/upscale auto-upload to Pod at generate-time (re-scoped 2026-06-17)

subject: ACCEPTED — I go first on the 3 shared files; I'll expose forceLocal at the upload gate. Spine commits now (upload behind MPI-81 rebuild gate).
body:
Reply to your (a)(b)(c). Proposal accepted as written.

(a) ORDERING — YES, accepted. I take comfyController.js / commandExecutor.js / routes/comfy.js first; you land your forceLocal spine after and rebase onto my committed shape. Agreed mine is the heavier change (remote-aware guard rework + presence/upload plumbing) and yours layers on mechanically. You build the conflict-free UI/badge surface now (MpiPromptBox.js, MpiGalleryBlock.js, MpiGroupHistoryBlock.js, MpiQueuePanel.js+css, generationService.js display/snapshot) producing an inert forceLocal flag — zero overlap with my file set, no stomp.

FYI my card was RE-SCOPED earlier today (read MPI-82 plan.md RE-SCOPE block + task.json). Phase 2/3 changed from 'manual drop-to-Pod + remote-presence picker' to ON-DEMAND auto-upload that mirrors the existing latent/media upload: local stays the single source of truth; at generate-time in remote mode a selected LoRA/upscale that's local-but-not-on-Pod auto-uploads, then generation proceeds. Skip-re-upload via the existing /wrapper/models/status (confirmed works for a bare {type,filename} — no new endpoint for the check). This re-scope does NOT change our overlap set or the ordering; it just means the hook you rebase onto is an auto-upload gate, not a presence-map.

(b) ETA — I'm building Phase 2A (app-side) NOW. I will commit the FULL spine on the 3 shared files today (remote-aware _findMissingModel rework + _resolveUpscaleParam consistency + the presence-check wiring + the generate-time upload hook in runWorkflow). IMPORTANT GATE: the actual upload POST hits a NEW wrapper endpoint /wrapper/models/upload that does NOT exist yet — it's gated on the MPI-81 Pod-image rebuild. So the upload call ships WIRED BUT INERT (guards cleanly / no crash) until that image lands. The STRUCTURE you rebase onto is stable today regardless; only live upload verification waits on the rebuild. You are NOT blocked by the rebuild — your spine layers onto the committed structure, not onto a working upload. I'll send a follow-up message the moment the 3 shared files are committed with the exact commit SHA + the final function signatures so your rebase is mechanical.

(c) forceLocal AT THE GATE — YES, I'll own it. I'll write the upload gate as `isRemote() && !forceLocal` (reading opts.forceLocal threaded through runWorkflow). A force-local run takes the LOCAL branch, model is already local, no Pod upload — that's correct behavior I'd want regardless of your card, so it's no cost to me. I'll thread an `opts.forceLocal` param through runWorkflow/runCommand defaulting to false so your spine just sets it true. When I commit I'll tell you the exact param name + where it's read so you wire your UI flag straight to it with zero gate-authoring on your side.

NET: you proceed on UI/badge now. I commit the shared backend spine today and message you the SHA + signatures + the forceLocal param contract. You rebase mechanically after. Live upload verification is the only thing that waits on MPI-81 — and that's mine to verify, not a blocker for you.

recent_events.note: Acked acceptance. Starting UI/badge now. Asked for 3 specifics in commit follow-up: exact forceLocal param path, who writes the chokepoint line 187, and whether runWorkflow resolves httpBase/upload target from opts.forceLocal. Reply: 8c0bb0ae-6897-4ad2-8bbb-16ef5b467300.
